_meta.status: SEED — illustrative library shapes, NOT confirmed from real captured workflows.
_meta.provenance_warning: Every entry is confirmed:false on purpose. These are realistic, format-correct mappings whose only job is to let the library-match mechanism (recipeUnitSource -> matchLibraryRecipes) resolve and rank a fitting recipe for a captured unit. They were authored, not derived from a real confirm-pass over actual captured-and-confirmed workflows. The enriched spec / seam_dims / lifecycle fields are likewise illustrative. BEFORE ANY PILOT: replace this file with the output of a real confirm-pass, and only then set confirmed:true. Do NOT present these to a client as confirmed/trusted recipes — that would misrepresent provenance, which the product forbids.
_meta.edition_2_enrichment: Each entry now carries (a) the 7-field spec (goal/context/constraints/acceptanceCriteria/decomposition/escalation/modelFit — Change 1 + E2), (b) seam_dims (the E1 three-dimension friction/latency/criticality, each low|medium|high) for connection/span units, and (c) a lifecycle stage (E4). Because every entry is confirmed:false, its lifecycle is 'captured' and its spec/seam_dims read as inferred provenance downstream — never stated. A real confirm-pass would set lifecycle to 'specified'+ and the spec fields to stated.
_meta.framing: All recipe text is leverage / assist-not-replace. A human-held unit keeps the person approving and wears the reserved Human Pink. The economics-token family is excluded by construction; cost-to-serve / model-fit language lives only in the spec.modelFit field (a recipe-surface field).
_meta.match_vocabulary: The match signatures key off the SAME shape inputs the Leverage Map detector already reads — NOT a parallel vocabulary. For a connection/span unit: 'motivators' is a subset of the detector's seam motivator kinds (shared-tool | manual-channel | system-switching | transition), 'handoff_kind' is one of HANDOFF_KINDS (role-to-role | human-to-system | system-to-human | system-to-system) and requires a 'transition' motivator, and the E1 'latency' / 'criticality' dims (low|medium|high) match the seam's three-dimension read. For a step unit: 'step_type' is one of STEP_TYPE_OPTIONS, 'friction' of FRICTION_KINDS, 'role' of ROLE_VALUES, 'decision' of DECISION_KINDS. 'human_held' mirrors the seam/step human-hold (held recipes fit held units only). 'tools_any' is a SOFT disambiguator (token overlap with the captured tool text); it never gates a fit, only ranks.

schema.id: stable identifier for the seed entry
schema.label: human-readable name of the workflow shape
schema.unit_kind: step | connection | span (Option A — a recipe unit is a step, a handoff, or a short span; a span entry fits a connection unit)
schema.match: the captured-shape signature, in the detector's real vocabulary, the matcher compares a unit against (now including the E1 latency / criticality seam dims)
schema.leverage: lo | md | hi — the rank used to order fitting recipes (never a percent, never an economics number)
schema.human_held: true if the unit involves judgment a person must own; the recipe then keeps the person approving and wears reserved Human Pink (#FF4FD8)
schema.seam_dims: E1 — { friction, latency, criticality } each low|medium|high, for connection/span units (illustrative until a real confirm-pass)
schema.spec: Change 1 + E2 — { goal, context, constraints, acceptanceCriteria, decomposition[], escalation, modelFit } (illustrative; inferred provenance until confirmed)
schema.lifecycle: E4 — captured | confirmed | specified | in-build | in-telemetry (always 'captured' here because confirmed:false)
schema.source: always 'example' in this file; becomes 'library' (confirmed) only after a real confirm-pass

entries.label: Export → manual reconciliation (controller close)
entries.recipe: AI assembles the reconciliation working file from both sources and surfaces the items that do not tie out, so the person reviews the exceptions instead of building the comparison by hand.

spec.goal: A reconciliation working file with exceptions surfaced, for the controller.
spec.context: Two source extracts (ERP + ledger); the person reviews the tie-out.
spec.constraints: Confidential financials; draft-only up to human review; approved tools only.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Ties to both sources; every non-tie is flagged, none silently dropped.
decomposition: Surface the items that do not tie out
spec.escalation: Hand back any unexplained variance above threshold to the controller.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier; confidential data stays on approved / in-VPC models. Cost-to-serve is a band; route to the cheapest tier that clears acceptance.

entries.label: Spreadsheet → ledger re-key (accounts payable)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the ledger entries from the source export and flags mismatches for review, so the re-keying between the two systems is carried for the person rather than done by hand.

spec.goal: Draft ledger entries from the source export, mismatches flagged.
spec.context: A spreadsheet export feeding the ledger; the person reviews before posting.
spec.constraints: Internal/confidential financials; draft-only up to human review.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Every entry traces to a source row; mismatches flagged, none posted unreviewed.
spec.escalation: Stop and hand back if a mismatch can't be explained from the source.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier; draft-only. Cost-to-serve is a band; route to the cheapest tier that clears acceptance.

entries.label: Invoice routed for approval (accounts payable)
entries.recipe: AI packages the invoice with its supporting detail and a recommended coding and routes it to the approver; the person approves. Never removes the approval step.

spec.goal: A packaged invoice + recommended coding routed to the approver.
spec.context: An invoice awaiting role-to-role approval; the person owns the decision.
spec.constraints: Internal financials; the approval is human-held; no auto-approval.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: The approver receives complete supporting detail; the decision stays a person's.
decomposition: Route to the approver for the decision
spec.escalation: AI never approves; a person approves before payment proceeds.
spec.modelFit: Judgment-adjacent → mid tier for the packaging; the decision stays human (no tier). Cost-to-serve is a band.

entries.label: Package the variance summary for review (controller close)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the variance summary from the reconciled figures, groups the items for review, and notes what moved, so the person spends their attention on which variances matter.

spec.goal: A grouped variance summary noting what moved, for review.
spec.context: Reconciled figures feed the summary; the person judges materiality.
spec.constraints: Confidential financials; draft-only up to human review.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Every material variance is grouped and explained; none omitted.
spec.escalation: Flag any variance the data can't explain to the reviewer.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier; confidential stays on approved / in-VPC models. Cost-to-serve is a band.

entries.label: Gather forecast inputs into the model (FP&A)
entries.recipe: AI gathers the forecast inputs from the source systems and assembles them into the model's input shape, so the person works on the assumptions rather than the collection.

spec.goal: Forecast inputs assembled into the model's input shape.
spec.context: Inputs span several source systems; the person owns the assumptions.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Every model input is populated from a sourced value; gaps flagged.
decomposition: Gather inputs across the source systems
Map them to the model's input shape
spec.escalation: Hand back to the analyst when an input can't be sourced.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier. Cost-to-serve is a band; route to the cheapest tier that clears acceptance.

entries.label: Flag overdue → collections outreach (accounts receivable)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the collections outreach for each overdue item and prepares it for sending, so the person reviews and sends rather than composing each message from scratch.

spec.goal: Drafted collections outreach per overdue item, ready to review and send.
spec.context: An aging report flags overdue items; the person reviews and sends.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Each draft cites the correct overdue item and amount; none sent unreviewed.
decomposition: Prepare for the person to review and send
spec.escalation: Hand back any disputed or sensitive account to the person.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier; draft-only. Cost-to-serve is a band.

entries.label: Assemble the daily cash position (treasury)
entries.recipe: AI assembles the daily cash position from the account feeds and highlights anything unusual, so the person reviews a prepared position instead of compiling it.

spec.goal: A daily cash position assembled from the account feeds, anomalies highlighted.
spec.context: Several bank/account feeds; the person reviews the prepared position.
spec.constraints: Confidential treasury data; approved tools only; draft-only.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Every account feed is reflected; anything unusual is highlighted.
spec.escalation: Flag a feed that didn't arrive or doesn't reconcile to the person.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier; confidential stays on approved / in-VPC models. Cost-to-serve is a band.

entries.label: Assess a control (SOX compliance)
entries.recipe: AI assembles and routes the control evidence with a draft assessment for review; the person makes the control assessment. Never removes the human assessment.

spec.goal: Control evidence assembled with a draft assessment, for the person's call.
spec.context: A SOX control under assessment; the person owns the conclusion.
spec.constraints: Confidential/regulated; the assessment is human-held; no auto-conclusion.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: All required evidence is attached; the human makes the assessment.
decomposition: Route to the person for the call
spec.escalation: AI never concludes the control; the person assesses and signs off.
spec.modelFit: Judgment-adjacent → mid tier for the packaging; the assessment stays human (no tier). Cost-to-serve is a band.

entries.label: Export → cross-check for stale deals (revenue operations)
entries.recipe: AI assembles the week-over-week comparison and proposes which deals look stale with the supporting signals; the person decides what counts as stale. Never removes the judgment.

spec.goal: A week-over-week comparison proposing stale deals, for the person's judgment.
spec.context: A CRM pipeline export; the person decides what counts as stale.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Each proposed-stale deal carries its supporting signals; the person decides.
decomposition: Route to the person to decide
spec.escalation: AI never marks a deal dead; the person decides.
spec.modelFit: Judgment-adjacent → mid tier; the decision stays human. Cost-to-serve is a band.

entries.label: Stale deals → owner messaging and leadership summary (revenue operations)
entries.recipe: AI drafts owner outreach for each flagged deal and the leadership summary and prepares them for sending, so the person reviews and routes rather than writing each one.

spec.goal: Drafted owner outreach + a leadership summary, ready to review and route.
spec.context: Flagged deals span CRM + comms; the person reviews and routes.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: Each draft names the correct deal/owner; the summary reconciles to the flags.
decomposition: Draft owner outreach per flagged deal
spec.escalation: Hand back any sensitive account to the person before sending.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier; draft-only. Cost-to-serve is a band.

entries.label: Intake request → route to category (procurement)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the intake summary and proposes the routing category from the request detail, so the person confirms and forwards rather than triaging from scratch.

spec.goal: An intake summary + a proposed routing category, for the person to confirm.
spec.context: A procurement request entering the system; the person confirms routing.
spec.constraints: Internal; draft-only; the person confirms before forwarding.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: The proposed category is justified from the request detail; the person confirms.
spec.escalation: Hand back an ambiguous request to the person to categorize.
spec.modelFit: Assembly → small tier. Cost-to-serve is a band; route to the cheapest tier that clears acceptance.

entries.label: Route an item for sign-off (general)
entries.recipe: AI packages the item with its supporting detail and routes it to the right approver with a recommended decision; the person approves. Never removes the sign-off.

spec.goal: A packaged item routed to the right approver with a recommended decision.
spec.context: An item awaiting role-to-role sign-off; the person owns the decision.
spec.constraints: The sign-off is human-held; no auto-approval.
spec.acceptanceCriteria: The approver gets complete supporting detail; the sign-off stays a person's.
spec.escalation: AI never signs off; the person approves before it proceeds.
spec.modelFit: Judgment-adjacent → mid tier for packaging; the sign-off stays human. Cost-to-serve is a band.
